title: Authorize Wireless Access Prior to Allowing Such Connections
description: authorize wireless access prior to allowing such connections

checks.name: Wireless Access Points are Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of wireless access points is a network inventory and architectural GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Client

checks.name: Wireless Access is Authorized Prior to Allowing Connections
checks.purpose: Verify that any active wireless adapter is connected only to authorized networks using WPA2 or WPA3 enterprise authentication and that unauthorized wireless profiles are not present

checks.name: Wireless Access Points are Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of wireless access points is a network inventory and architectural GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Server

checks.name: Wireless Access is Authorized Prior to Allowing Connections
checks.purpose: Verify that wireless adapters are disabled on the server or if present are connected only to authorized networks with enterprise authentication, and that Group Policy restricts unauthorized wireless connections

checks.name: Wireless Access Points are Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of wireless access points is a network inventory and architectural GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Linux

checks.name: Wireless Access is Authorized Prior to Allowing Connections
checks.purpose: Verify that any active wireless interface is connected to an authorized network using WPA2 or WPA3 enterprise authentication and that unauthorized wireless interfaces are disabled or absent

checks.name: Wireless Access Points are Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of wireless access points is a network inventory and architectural GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Debian

checks.name: Wireless Access is Authorized Prior to Allowing Connections
checks.purpose: Verify that any active wireless interface is connected to an authorized network using WPA2 or WPA3 enterprise authentication and that unauthorized wireless interfaces are disabled or absent
